MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. THI HOANG HO, Esq. of Dykema Gossett LLP (Tel: 213-457-1800) for Tina Tran (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ALEXANDER M. WEYAND, Esq. of Weyand Law Firm (Tel: 415-536-2800) for Tina Tran and Ngoc My Vuong (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. BRETT D. SCHWEINBERG, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran, LLP (Tel: 415-673-5600) for Mary Anne Baysac and Jim Harvey Sy;
Atty. ANDREW J. GOLDMAN, Esq. of the Goldman Law Firm (Tel: 415-435-5500) for Mary Anne Baysac, D.D.S. (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TINA TRAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE NOS. CGC-21-596341 AND CGC-21-592890 WITH CASE NO. CGC-20-588778.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Tina Tran's motion to consolidate cases 596341 and 592890 with 588778 is granted for all purposes. Tran and Mary Ann Baysac are the two key figures in all three cases. The latter case involves a dental-business partnership dispute between the pair. The former cases involve a condominium Tran and her mother bought from Baysac and her husband so, Tran says, Tran could be closer to the dental businesses she worked at with Baysac. Tran further asserts that the condo sale would not have been as favorable to Baysac but for their business relationship.

Efficiency would be served, among other ways, by taking depositions of the key witnesses once; otherwise, the same common facts would be covered multiple times. (CCP 1048(a).) Also, court resources would be preserved by not holding multiple trials. Moreover, Baysac's opposition does not claim she would be prejudiced by consolidation.

All future papers shall be filed in case 588778.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
